entry.title: Re: Introducing Support Content — the additional Customer Support knowledge repository
entry.content: Thanks for sharing Marcelo!!

entry.title: Re: Blog#2: How Cross Landscape Distribution (XLD) can be integrated into SAP Cloud ALM
entry.content:
Hi @pbalkrishnan, that's actually one of the use cases of Cross-Landscape-Distribution (XLD) . You can define a list of customizing tables (down to the key) that are relevant for the synchronization between S/4HANA and MDG.  It is very similar to what you might know from SAP Solution Manager.

And the XLD solution that you see in the video is part of pony!

entry.title: Re: Blog#1: Finally it is here - How "pony" brings custom fields to SAP Cloud ALM
entry.content: Hi @Neha_Singh601, you need to deploy pony in your BTP subaccount and connected it with destinations to your SAP Cloud ALM tenant and your IAS.

entry.title: Re: How to handle Data Attributes Color and Volume
entry.content:
When the exact color is not available in the code list, the customer should choose the closest match - for lilac or purple that would be either RED or BLUE, depending on the nuance of the color. Leaving the color empty (null) may lead to wrong report fraction assignment.

Most regulations check for the most frequently used colors:

- Clear (natural) for all plastics

- Light Blue (LBL) for PET

- Green / Brown more often for glass, less often for plastic

- Black (for plastic and glass) - usually results in a penalty

Customers can open feature request for adding more colors or vote on existing requests for more colors.

Volume is required for hollow body containers - Bottles (BTL), Jars (JAR), Cans (CAN), Brick (BRK), Flask (FLA), 

It may be required for some other packaging types that could hold liquids or gases, like IBC, Tub (TUB), Tube (TBE), Aerosol container (GAC), Buckets (BCK), Cup (CUP) but these are seen less often in reports.

The generic container type, CNR, should also have volume, because some reports include CNR with the hollow-body containers.

We haven't seen a case where volume would be required for packaging that is "shallow" and rarely holds liquids - like TRY, TBW, Cage, Box, etc.

Due to EU Single-use plastic directive (SUP) all EU countries require the volume for beverage bottles and cups for beverages.

Customers should put the volume of the product that's inside the container, as it's printed on the label.

Obviously a bottle for 1.5L of beverage needs to have volume a little more than 1.5L, because of the empty space, but from our experience, PROs expect to see 1.5L in this case.

In the unlikely event, when the container has a lot of empty space (e.g 1L liquid in 3L container), the company should request guidance from the PRO how to maintain volume in this case.

entry.title: Re: SAP Document AI Workspace: Features You Need to Know
entry.content: Good one 👍

entry.title: Flexible Workflow – Multiple Workflow Determination for Purchasing Group and Material Group
entry.content:
Our business requirement is to maintain two different approval groups:

* Approval Group 1: Based on Purchasing Group (e.g., VMU) with Approver 1 and Approver 2.
* Approval Group 2: Based on Material Group with Approver 3 and Approver 4.

For specific Material Groups, the workflow should be routed only to the Material Group approvers (Approver 3 and Approver 4). The Purchasing Group workflow should not be triggered for these Material Groups.

However, when we configure both workflows, both are triggered because the Purchasing Group condition is also satisfied.

entry.title: Re: Understanding HANA memory related license utilization in SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition
entry.content:
Dear @carlos_junior3 ,

Claudia's blog is specifically covering the License calculation in SAP HANA S/4HANA Public Cloud (Also known as SAP Cloud ERP). The monitoring view you are referring to are not relevant for this product. 

When it comes to setting the PAL and License, I believe it will very much depends on the SAP product you are running on top of HANA. But again this is a topic that is completely outside of this blog's scope. 

Clément Mugner

entry.title: Re: Decimal of USD in SAP public cloud
entry.content:
Dear @Moon3,

This is standard system behavior. The Amount field in Manage Purchasing Info Records only allows a maximum of 2 decimal places, so a value such as 0.025 PLN / PC cannot be maintained directly and will be rounded. SAP confirms this limitation for the app and also mentions changing the price unit of measure as one of the options to avoid the issue

More details below:

SAP KBA 3598661 - Limitation of Decimal Places in Amount Field in App Manage Purchasing Info Records

The recommended approach is to maintain the same commercial price with a larger price unit, for example:

0.025 PLN / 1 PC

should be maintained as:

25.00 PLN / 1000 PC

or

2.50 PLN / 100 PC

This keeps the same price mathematically, but avoids entering more than 2 decimals in the currency amount field

More details below:

SAP also confirms that the per-unit price in Purchase Orders is restricted to 2 decimal places and that higher precision is currently not available in the standard system

SAP KBA 3733837 - Purchase Order Pricing Precision Limited to 2 Decimal Places

It is also not possible in Public Cloud to change the standard decimal-place setting for currency amounts, for example changing PLN from 2 decimals to 3 or more decimals

More details below:

SAP KBA 3632144 - Limitation of extending Decimal Places for Currency Amounts in SAP S/4HANA Public Cloud

So for this case, please maintain the purchasing price using a higher price unit for example 25.00 PLN per 1000 PC and validate the result in the PO, goods receipt and supplier invoice process. Please also note that final document amounts in PLN will still be rounded according to the standard PLN currency decimals, so very small quantities may still lead to rounding differences

Best Regards,

Dawid

entry.title: Re: Extra objects being populated in the Calibration PM O...
entry.content: Hello. I just tried checking in IP03. The Object list item tab is empty. There are no change documents as well.

entry.title: Recreating Excel‑Like Heat Maps in SAP Analytics Cloud Using Dynamic User‑Driven Thresholds
entry.content:
If you’ve worked with Excel heat maps, you already know how effortless it feels.

Just select your data → apply Conditional Formatting → Color Scales, and instantly you get a beautiful gradient showing highs, lows, and patterns. The colors automatically react to the values simple, intuitive, and powerful.

Excel :- 

But when you try to replicate the same behavior in SAP Analytics Cloud (SAC), things start to get interesting.

The Challenge in SAC

 In SAC, you can create heat maps and apply conditional formatting using thresholds. However:

Heat map charts rely on a single measure for color intensity
Conditional formatting in tables works with:
      -  Number ranges (static) OR
     -   Comparison to another measure (dynamic)
The real limitation comes when:  You want the gradient to adapt dynamically based on user-defined thresholds, like min/mid/max inputs similar to Excel flexibility.

Out-of-the-box, SAC doesn’t directly provide this level of interactive gradient control in a native heat map.

 So the question becomes:

Can we mimic Excel’s behavior using SAC building blocks?

Yes, and that’s exactly what this solution does.

 Solution Overview

 Instead of relying on the native SAC heat map, the approach shifts to:

          Using a Table + Calculated Measure + Conditional Formatting

This gives us full control over how colors are applied

 Step 1: Design the Data Structure

In the main SAC table:

Measure X → Holds the actual values (the data you want to visualize)
Values are stored at specific intersections (e.g., based on dimensions like Bay, Post, etc.)
Additionally, create 3 input measures:

  • Minimum
  • Midpoint
  • Maximum

These are not part of the main data grid, but stored at separate intersections where users can input values

Step 2: Enable User Input for Thresholds

Users enter:

  •  Minimum threshold
  •  Midpoint threshold
  •  Maximum threshold

This allows complete flexibility; users can dynamically control how the gradient behaves based on their scenario.

Step 3: Create the Logic – “XColorScore”

1) Created a Restricted Measure "RM-X" restricted  to its intersection at a a story level 

2) Created 3 Restricetd Measures one for each Measure restricted to its intersection at a story level

  •  Minimum (RM - Min)
  •  Maximum (RM - Max)
  •  Midpoint (RM - Mid)

3) Create a calculated measure (XColorScore) that:

Compares each Measure X value against Minimum, Midpoint, and Maximum

Example logic (conceptually):

If X <= Minimum → assign lower score
If X ≈ Midpoint → assign medium score
If X >= Maximum → assign higher score
Essentially, you convert your raw values into a color scoring scale.

This follows the SAC best practice of deriving a numeric output to drive conditional formatting

 

Step 4: Apply Conditional Formatting

Now comes the clever part.

In SAC:

  • Select Measure X 
  • Go to Conditional Formatting (Thresholds)
          Choose:  Compare To → Measure 
                            Comparison Measure → XColorScore

Since thresholds in SAC can compare one measure against another, this enables dynamic, data-driven coloring logic instead of static ranges.

 

Step 5: Achieve the Gradient Effect

By defining appropriate ranges in the threshold:

Green → lower values
Yellow → mid-range
Red → higher values
You effectively simulate:

  •  A heat map
  • A gradient scale
  • Based on user inputs


Why This Approach Works

This design leverages three powerful SAC concepts:

Separation of Data & Logic
→ Raw data (X) vs Colour logic (XColorScore)
User-driven input
→ Thresholds are not hardcoded
Measure-to-measure comparison
→ Unlocks dynamic conditional formatting

 

 Key Takeaways

Native SAC heat maps are great, but not always flexible enough
Conditional formatting with measure comparison is the real game changer
By introducing a scoring layer, you can simulate Excel-like gradients
Most importantly:  You give control back to the business user

Closing Thoughts

Moving from Excel to SAC often exposes these small gaps in flexibility.

But with a bit of creative modelling, SAC can go beyond Excel,especially when you combine:

  • Planning input
  •  Calculated measures
  •  Conditional formatting

This solution is a perfect example of that.

entry.title: Re: Data Migration for SmartRecruiters
entry.content:
Hi Raju,
Great questions — this is exactly the stuff worth nailing down before you start.

 

1) How to migrate the requisitions (jobs) from your 3rd-party tool
The golden rule: only bring over your active / currently open jobs. Don't try to drag your whole history across — it just clutters the new system. You've got three ways to get those jobs into SR:

  1. Have recruiters manually recreate them (best for a handful of jobs)
  2. Use a job sync / migration, or
  3. Push them in via the Job API (best for larger volumes).

When you import, map the fields to your new SR setup rather than recreating old custom fields — let your future-state config drive the mapping. Each job needs the basics: title, location, description, industry, function, employment level, and an Open Date (tip: enter the real/past open date so your time-to-hire reporting stays accurate).
2) How to handle requisitions that are "in progress"
This is really about the candidates sitting inside those open jobs. The approach depends on how far along they are:

Jobs in the offer stage → only migrate the finalist(s). Everyone else can be dispositioned in the old system.
Other in-process jobs → migrate the active candidates (anyone not rejected / past a certain stage), or invite them to reapply.
Brand-new jobs → don't migrate at all; just bulk-email candidates a custom apply link.

You can either mass-load candidates manually (download their resumes → drag-and-drop into the SR job → set the right Source, Status, and Notes), or import them through the Candidate API. One heads-up: candidates moved this way will show as no consent given (since they didn't apply directly), and diversity info can't be added via manual import — so loop in your legal team and consider a bulk consent request. Also, you can update the "nice-to-have" data after go-live — it shouldn't block your launch.
3) Ensuring data transfers cleanly during go-live (cutover)
The key tool here is a "freeze" period — you pause recruiting in the old tool while you cut over. The clean recipe:

Schedule the freeze and tell everyone recruiting is paused.
In the legacy tool, review and disposition all open applicants.
Close out any jobs you can in the old system.
Recreate and publish the still-open jobs in SR, then mass-load the active candidates into them.
Start opening all new jobs in SR going forward.

Two practical tips: before you load everything, temporarily switch off approvals, automated emails, and workflow automation in SR so you don't accidentally spam candidates and hiring teams mid-migration (then switch them back on after). And remember for reporting — your legacy system covers everything up to the freeze date, SR covers everything after, so you may need to combine reports for jobs that spanned both.

SmartRecruiters API reference (the relevant endpoints for the above):

Developer home / overview — https://developers.smartrecruiters.com/docs/customer-overview
Job API (extract/import jobs i.e. requisitions) — covered under the Customer API overview above; reference: https://developers.smartrecruiters.com/docs/api-reference
Candidate API (import, export, read, update candidates & applications) — https://developers.smartrecruiters.com/docs/permission-reference
Application API (submit applications + screening/diversity questions + consent) — https://developers.smartrecruiters.com/docs/application-api-1
Posting API (published jobs) — https://developers.smartrecruiters.com/docs/endpoints
Get started / auth (API Key vs OAuth 2.0) — https://developers.smartrecruiters.com/docs/get-started

entry.title: Re: Connecting SAP SuccessFactors and SAP Datasphere
entry.content:
Hey, thanks a lot for this guide—it’s really helpful.

Even though we’ve uploaded the root certificate,


we’re still getting an error message stating that the certificate is invalid.


I did notice, however, that our certificate has a different name than the ones listed in the documentation. Instead of "DigiCert Global Root CA," our root certificate is named "Digital Guardian, Inc."

Do you have any tips on how to find the cause of the problem, or at least narrow it down?

entry.title: Re: Business partner 100231 does not exist in the standar...
entry.content: The issue is not with the account group config itself, but with missing credit management role/data for the domestic BP.  MKK is the role category for Customer Credit Management in SAP S/4HANA. This means the BP used for the Domestic Account Group (ZDOM) is missing the Credit Management role (UKM000 / FLCUCR) or it is not properly maintained for the company code/plant you are working with. For ZFRN, the BP likely has the correct roles assigned (Customer role + Credit Management role), so the sales order creation succeeds.

entry.title: Vendor Evaluation with AI Prompt Engineering in SAP AI
entry.content:
By Satish Vallabhaneni and Raunak Bhatia

Introduction:

 In today’s fast-paced procurement landscape, every delayed vendor onboarding costs your business time and money. While traditional automation handles structured data well, managing unstructured supplier emails, diverse document formats, and initial risk checks still requires heavy manual effort. Enter the Prompt Editor within the SAP AI Core Generative AI Hub. By leveraging this powerful tool, you can build, test, and deploy tailored AI prompts that automatically extract vendor details, draft introductory emails, and trigger onboarding workflows in seconds. In this blog post, we will walk through how to utilize the Prompt Editor to orchestrate a seamless, low-effort vendor onboarding process that integrates directly with your existing SAP landscape.

 

Procurement teams spend enormous time manually reviewing vendor documents — financial statements, compliance certificates, insurance documents, reference letters — before onboarding a new supplier or doing annual risk reviews. In large enterprises this can take 3–7 days per vendor, and with hundreds of vendors onboarded annually, the cumulative cost is massive.

With the Prompt Editor's multi-modal capability (text + document upload), you can demo a live scenario where a raw vendor document goes in and a structured risk assessment narrative comes out in seconds — something that would normally take a sourcing analyst half a day.

Quantifiable business impact to present:

  • Vendor onboarding cycle time reduced from days to hours
  • Risk assessment cost per vendor reduced by 60–75%
  • Consistent, auditable risk scoring (removes human subjectivity)
  • Compliance team bandwidth freed for exception handling only

 

Execution:

Step-by-Step: How to Demo This in SAP AI Launchpad Prompt Editor

Step 1 — Open Prompt Editor
Navigate to SAP AI Launchpad → Generative AI Hub → Prompt Editor

 

 

Step 2 — Select a Multi-modal Model
From the Model Library, select a model that supports document/image input such as Gemini 2.5 Create a deployment if not already active. This is critical because you'll be uploading vendor documents directly.

 

 

 

Step 3 — Prepare a Sample Vendor Document
Use a dummy or anonymized vendor document — ideally a 1–2 page PDF containing:

  • Company registration details
  • Annual turnover figures
  • Compliance certifications (ISO, SOC2 etc.)
  • Bank/financial reference

 

Sample document in PDF format

 

 

 

Step 4 — Set the System Prompt
In the System prompt section of the editor, define the AI's role clearly:

"You are a Procurement Risk Analyst at a large enterprise. When given a vendor document, you will extract key risk indicators and produce a structured vendor risk summary with: (1) Vendor Profile, (2) Financial Risk Rating (Low/Medium/High), (3) Compliance Status, (4) Key Red Flags if any, (5) Recommended Next Action. Keep the output concise and suitable for a Procurement Manager review."

 

 

Step 5 — Upload the Vendor Document
Use the Upload Image/Document button in the Prompt Editor interface, attach your sample vendor PDF, and add a simple user message:

"Please analyse this vendor document and generate the risk narrative."

 

 

Step 6 — Tune Parameters

  • Set Temperature to 0.1–0.2 for consistent, factual output
  • Set Max Tokens to 500–800 to keep the narrative concise but complete

Step 7 — Run and Review Output
Hit Run and show stakeholders the structured risk narrative generated in seconds. Compare it side-by-side with how long a manual review would take.

 

 

 

Step 8 — Test with Contrast Scenarios
Run the same prompt against two vendor documents — one clean, one with a red flag (e.g., missing certification, low turnover). Show how the AI correctly differentiates risk levels. This is the moment that impresses the room.

 

 

 

 

Step 9 — Save to Prompt Registry
Save the validated prompt as a versioned template in Prompt Management, so Procurement, Legal, and Compliance teams all use the same standardised assessment logic — removing inconsistency across analysts.

Step 10 — Present the ROI
Build a simple metric: if the company onboards 300 vendors/year, and each manual review takes 4 hours at an analyst cost of ₹800/hour, that's ₹9.6 lakhs per year in manual effort — most of which gets eliminated. Add speed-to-onboard improvement and you have a compelling CFO-ready number.

 

Closing:

Streamlining your vendor onboarding process does not require a complete overhaul of your procurement systems; it simply requires adding a layer of intelligent automation. By mastering the Prompt Editor in the SAP AI Core Generative AI Hub, you transform a traditionally slow, manual chore into an instant, automated workflow. This not only cuts down on administrative errors but also allows your procurement teams to focus on strategic supplier relationships rather than data entry. As you take your next steps into generative AI, try building a basic extraction prompt using your own vendor templates—you will be amazed at how quickly you can turn raw supplier data into a ready-to-use business asset.

entry.title: Re: Unabel to do Partial capitalization in KO02
entry.content:
Dear Customer,

The error is coming because the settlement rule is maintained with settlement type FUL.

For FUL / Actual Settlement, SAP expects the total receiver percentage to be 100%. In your case, only 50% has been maintained, so the system is stopping you with the message:

“Total percent = 50% (Set. Type FUL / Version / Actual settlement)”

You may refer to this link for more information

This is standard behavior. SAP needs to know where the full amount should be settled. If only 50% is assigned to an asset, the remaining 50% has no receiver, so the settlement rule cannot be saved.

If you want to split the capitalization, you need to maintain another receiver line so that the total becomes 100%. For example:

Asset 1 = 50%
Asset 2 / Cost Center / other valid receiver = 50%

However, if your requirement is to capitalize only 50% now and keep the remaining 50% on AuC for later, then this should not be done by maintaining only 50% in the FUL rule. You may need to review the settlement design, such as periodic/preliminary settlement or the correct receiver setup, depending on your business process.

In short, for settlement type FUL, the total percentage must always be 100%. Partial capitalization does not mean that the FUL rule can be maintained with only 50%.

I hopes this help!

entry.title: Re: The status of item category SVS4 in the Service Order...
entry.content:
Good day @ISoma 

Thank you for your question.

Based on your description and the item category SVS4, the behaviour you experienced with item 20 is expected.

Reason is, SVS4 is a non-stock service part that is procured externally. Releasing the service order created the purchase requisition, and the subsequent purchase order and goods receipt complete the procurement step. However, the goods receipt does not by itself complete the lifecycle of the service order item. Service confirmations are used to record the actual consumption of service order items.

Here are references for more guidance.

https://help.sap.com/docs/SAP_S4HANA_CLOUD/adbae5bcd5994f159bf2847a11397b61/3a2f024ad33345f393ef8b9f3bf0a858.html?locale=en-US

https://help.sap.com/docs/SAP_S4HANA_CLOUD/adbae5bcd5994f159bf2847a11397b61/f51f9127ec904db69be5bfc40cfcaeb4.html?locale=en-US

So, for you to change item 20 from Released to Completed, a service confirmation must be created for the remaining quantity, marked as Final Confirmation, and itself set to Completed. See KBA 3717692 for more insights.

Here is a quick guide:

In Manage Service Orders, check the document flow for item 20 and confirm a service order already exists.

If none exists, create one for the procured quantity, select item 20 in the confirmation, enter your quantity that corresponds to the received and consumed quantity, set the final confirmation, change the service confirmation status to completed and refresh the service order.

Ensure you do not create multiple final confirmation for the same item. If the complete quantity was already consumed by an earlier confirmation that was later cancelled or changed, the remaining confirmable quantity may be zero. Should that be the case consult KBA 3717692 on how to proceed.

 

Let me know if you have further question.

Best regards

Chris

entry.title: Re: SAP S/4HANA – SAP Note 1489799 for BAPI_PR_CREATE (Su...
entry.content: My suggestion is before applying SAP Note 1489799, which is related to ECC, raise a ticket to SAP referencing your S/4HANA release. They will confirm whether a successor note exists or if you should use a workaround. Alternatively, use transaction SNOTE or SAP ONE Support Launchpad to see if 1489799 is flagged as “valid in S/4HANA” or replaced.

entry.title: Re: Decoupling transport from feature
entry.content:
Hi Shivani.

The unassign option of a transport request from a Feature will allow you to work with the Feature without taking in consideration de status of this unassign transport. This unassign X is like a decouple.

Best regards,

Dolores

entry.title: Re: SAP S/4HANA – SAP Note 1489799 for BAPI_PR_CREATE (Su...
entry.content:
Hello @Jimmy6 

The note 1489799 - ME 154: Creating subcontracting purchase requisition w/o BOM is not valid for S/4HANA. It is valid only for:

The note is included in the packages:

The corrections of the note are already provided in S/4HANA.

The bottom line is - you can't implement the note in S/4HANA as its corrections are already there.

Best regards

Dominik Tylczynski

entry.title: Re: While Defining Multilevel PO Release Strategy, Boxes...
entry.content:
To answer your questions

1) Why the prerequisite checkboxes are not appearing?
The prerequisite checkboxes only show up if the release codes are correctly assigned to the same release group and the system recognizes them as sequential (not parallel). In your RS2 setup, the system is treating codes 10 and 50 as parallel approvals, so no dependency matrix is generated and hence the blank screen.

2) Is there any configuration step that might have been missed?

Yes, common misses include:

  • Release Group assignment: Both codes must belong to the same release group.
  • Release Strategy definition: Codes must be entered in sequence under the strategy, not just listed.
  • Release Code linkage: Each release code must be defined and active in customizing (SPRO → MM → Purchasing → Purchase Order → Release Procedure → Define Release Codes).
  • Release Prerequisites table (T16FS): If entries aren’t generated, the system won’t show checkboxes.

3) How can I make the prerequisite checkboxes visible?
Recreate RS2 ensuring codes 10 and 50 are assigned to the same release group and strategy. Save the strategy, then revisit the prerequisites tab. The system should now generate the checkbox matrix. If simulation still shows codes in a single line, adjust the strategy so the system interprets them as sequential releases (e.g., 10 must release before 50).

entry.title: Re: Extra objects being populated in the Calibration PM O...
entry.content:
Greetings,

can you check if anything is defined in:

SPRO -> Plant Maintenance and Customer Service -> System Enhancements and Data Transfer -> Business Add-Ins -> BAdI: Generating Object List in Maintenance Orders

Best regards, M.

entry.title: SAP Ariba Invoicing: France e-invoicing mandate from September 2026
entry.content:
Integration of French invoices via SAP Document and Reporting Compliance (PA)

SAP Ariba Invoicing, in conjunction with SAP Document and Reporting Compliance, offers an integrated and automated solution for SAP customers to comply with the French e-invoicing mandate, which will be rolled out in September 2026. Both the incoming invoices and the mandatory lifecycle statuses (REFUSÉE B2B) are supported:

The joint solution integrates invoices issued by French suppliers from their selected Plateforme Agréée (PA) and receives them into SAP Document and Reporting Compliance, cloud edition, which acts as the technology for SAP as a certified provider ( link ). The solution supports buyer-mandated lifecycle statuses, such as Refused, and sends these status updates back to the supplier's PA and the Public Platform (PPF).

Integration of French invoices via a third-party PA

Customers that do not use SAP Document and Reporting Compliance as their buyer PA can still comply with the French e-invoicing mandate by using the standard SAP Ariba Invoicing APIs. The receipt of invoices and the mandatory lifecycle statuses (B2B refusé) are supported by the solution.

This alternative option requires the following integration steps:

  1. Integration from the buyer PA: E-invoices received from the buyer's PA in any of the three formats compliant with the European Norm (UBL, CII, or Factur-X) must be converted or mapped to the SAP Ariba Invoicing invoice format by using the Supplier Invoices with Structured Data API.

  2. The rejection (refusal) status of rejected (refused) invoices in SAP Ariba Invoicing must be channelled back to the buyer’s PA. This can be done through integration by leveraging the Invoice Status Changes API. This service triggers calls to an external API each time there is an invoice status or payment status change from SAP Ariba Invoicing. In the context of the French e-invoicing mandate, only invoices moving to the Rejected status need to be channelled back to the buyer PA along with their rejection reason.

For more details on activating and using these SAP Ariba Invoicing APIs, please refer to the SAP Ariba Invoicing documentation for your deployment type:

  • Customer-managed (4N6): API Guide or
  • SAP-managed / Next-Gen SAP Ariba Invoicing (7YH): API Guide

entry.title: Re: Vendor Evaluation with AI Prompt Engineering in SAP AI
entry.content: Very useful document and detailed one

entry.title: Assistance Required for Debugging Cloud Build APK
entry.content:
Hi Experts,

We have built a custom client using Cloud Build. Could you please guide us on how to perform debugging using the Cloud Build APK?

Your assistance would be greatly appreciated.

Thank you.

entry.title: Re: Note 3754927 S4CORE
entry.content:
Hello Sir,

Based on the correction instruction, SAP Note 3754927 appears to be valid for S4CORE release 109, up to support package level SAPK-10901INS4CORE.

Therefore, from my current understanding, this note does not seem to be applicable for S4CORE version 108.

If the same or similar issue is occurring in a S4CORE 108 system, I believe it would be better to handle it through an open ticket for further analysis. Development may need to check whether the issue is also relevant for release 108, or whether a separate correction/backport is required.

I hope this helps

entry.title: UI5 component not loading in BTP Trial – app-preview error
entry.content:
Hi Experts, 
 
I am getting the below error for all applications in my BTP Trial account: "Failed to load UI5 component for navigation intent #app-preview" Apps are not opening from Launchpad. Attaching the same for the reference.
 
Could you please help what could be the issue?
 
Thanks.
 
 
SAP BTP, Cloud Foundry runtime and environment SAP Fiori #SAP UI

entry.title: Re: Is there any program to synch the ACDOCP cost element...
entry.content:
Hello Akesh, 

The program R_FINS_PLAN_TRANS_CO_S4H_2_ERP is the relevant standard program to transfer plan data from ACDOCP to classic CO tables. However, based on SAP KBA 3356231, secondary cost element category 42 is not supported by this program. Therefore, the error message received during transfer is expected from the current standard behavior, unless there is a specific correction note/downport available for the customer’s S/4HANA release.

For category 42 values, there does not appear to be another generic standard synchronization program that can simply transfer these ACDOCP values to classic CO tables. If the business process requires this, the case should remain open for further SAP checks, especially to confirm whether any release-specific correction or downport is available.

I hope this helps!

entry.title: Re: ABAP Cloud Developer Trial 2023 Available Now
entry.content:
Hi @joao_sousa2 , hi everyone,

Yes, I appreciate the frustration and am very conscious that everyone is being very patient here.

But I can only reiterate: As soon as I have anything to offer, I will update this blog post (as well as probably write a new, dedicated one.

Thanks to you all for your support,

Julie Plummer
ABAP PM.

entry.title: Re: Read the order long text before save in IW31/creation
entry.content:
Did you

  • Use a FM such as CO_ZK_TEXTKEY_CAUFV to build the TDNAME key.
  • Debug memory for MEMORY ID 'SAPLSTXD'
  • Debug function group STXD for some data defined in LSTXDTOP (local_key, ttxin_buffer, g_reftab, etc.)

entry.title: Re: Issue in PS Module - Billing of activities with quant...
entry.content: The discrepancy is caused by the decimal precision defined for SKM in CUNI. Updating SKM to allow two decimals will resolve the issue across PS confirmations, but it is not recommended to alter the standard unit definition. The best practice is to create a custom UoM with the required decimal precision and use that in your PS process.

entry.title: Re: Not able to find the default Event Mesh in Entitlemen...
entry.content: You need to look within Integration Suite.

entry.title: Re: Alert for pending Depreciation Run before closing a p...
entry.content:
Hi,

There is no direct link between Depreciation run and closing the period. If you want a control then use the Advanced Financial close (AFC) in S4  Hana where you list all the period end, quarter end and Year end closing steps. After all the necessary period end programs or steps are done  in AFC then you can close the period as last step of AFC.

entry.title: Re: Is there any program to synch the ACDOCP cost element...
entry.content:
Hi Akesh,

As far as I see this question should be answered with KBA 3356231.

The transfer of secondary costs from assessments is not possible with report R_FINS_PLAN_TRANS_CO_S4H_2_ERP. You'd need to transfer the plan data which is the basis for the assessment and execute the plan assessment then with the classic transactions if you need the secondary plan costs on the classic CO tables.

Kind regards

entry.title: Re: Unable to Capture PO Number from Success Message in T...
entry.content:
Good day @Murali96 

Thank you for your question.

Based on your screenshots, the issue appears to be related to using the complete dynamic success message as the Label of the Read action. Because the purchase order number changes with every execution, the recorded UI element may no longer match the value displayed during the next run.

The expressions (.*) and (*) did not work in your test. Therefore, you should not assume that the Label field supports regular expression-based substring extraction unless this is confirmed by SAP for the specific action and application.

The recommended approach is to record the Read action against a stable UI element that contains the purchase order number. Do not manually enter the complete success message or add regex characters to the Label unless the recorder automatically generates them.

If you cannot select the PO number separately from the transient success message, a practical way you can work around this is:

  1. Complete the purchase order creation.
  2. Open the created purchase order.
  3. Record a Read action on the stable PO number field.
  4. Bind the returned value to the variable PO_NUMBER.
  5. Reuse PO_NUMBER in the subsequent process step.

See the following reference for more insights.

https://help.sap.com/docs/SAP_S4HANA_CLOUD/1e9ba004e0504506a135afda960e9495/14d151d0e93f4688a434aba0f42bef47.html?locale=en-US

https://help.sap.com/docs/SAP_S4HANA_CLOUD/1e9ba004e0504506a135afda960e9495/67df844f5c204770accae88b35073374.html?locale=en-US

The PR process may work because the recorder can identify the PR number field differently from the PO success message. You should confirm this by comparing the recorded element labels and values in both scripts.

If the recorder cannot identify a separate PO number field, even after opening the PO, raise an incident and provide available details.

 

Best regards

Chris

entry.title: SAP DRC RFC oAuth2 code 404
entry.content:
Hello the community,

First time here and I'm asking to myself if someone has the same case than me.

I did all the configuration for SAP DRC relevant for our SAP ECC6 EHP8 (SP24) system.

The connection from Cloud Connector to the backend is OK and the ressource is available. The connection from BTP to the backend through the dedicated destination is OK when I test it. My issue is located on the created RFC type G I had to create with the oAuth2 configuration. I did all the chapter "Managing RFC Connections for SAP Document and Reporting Compliance, Cloud Edition" of the documentation : https://help.sap.com/docs/cloud-edition/sap-document-and-reporting-compliance-cloud-edition/managing-rfc-connections-for-sap-document-and-reporting-compliance-cloud-edition?locale=en-US

I first met two scenarios.

  • When I first tested the connetion, I met an error Create failed: plugin not active

So I checked note https://me.sap.com/notes/0003412080/E without success.

  • Then, I figured out that I didn't have both certificate G3/G5 in my STRUST. So I put them and I've retested the connection. I went from Create failed: plugin not active to error code 404 and not 401 as mentioned in the documentation

I really don't know where I failed. In SE80, all is done for the dedicated package.

In OA2C_CONFIG, I mentioned the clientID, clientSecret and the related URLs.

Authorization Endpoint : <enterprise>-drc-cloud-dev.authentication.eu02.hana.ondemand.com/oauth/authorization

Token Endpoint : <enterprise>-drc-cloud-dev.authentication.eu02.hana.ondemand.com/oauth/token

And the RFC configuration as been done as described...

Type : G

Host : drcc-test.c-257d320.kyma.ondemand.com

Path Prefix : /

Port : 443

Logon with User : Do not use a user

=> OAuth Settings => OA2C profile selected

Security Options, SSL Active

=> SSL Certificate ANONYM SSL Client, in which the ISRG Root X1 certificate has been set.

All the relevant oAuth2 SICF services seem to be activated.

The report OA2C_GENERIC_ACCESS return seems to be OK.

I'm still checking with SAP through a dedicated case but I don't know if I'm well understanded.

Thank you for your time and thank you if someone can help.

Have a nice day.

Regards.

Axel .

entry.title: Re: Partner user cannot see Partnership section in Sap4Me
entry.content: Hello, I am the Partner User Administrator and I posted this because I do not know what permissions must be granted for the final users's role. As I have already written, I assigned Partnership Basics and according to documentation this should be enough, but apparently it isn't.

entry.title: Re: Extra objects being populated in the Calibration PM O...
entry.content: Hello. I tried checking the SPRO and there is nothing. Also there have been orders which were created correctly without any extra objects last year. The issue orders were created in Feb this year and I could see the issue keeps happening even now.

entry.title: Re: Overcoming Challenges in Staffing Customer Projects with SAP Project and Resource Management
entry.content:
Hello Egbert,

The Resource Management capability of SAP Project and Resource Management complements the SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition Project capabilities and provides advanced staffing capabilities for Resource Managers. The integration scope item 6JO visualizes in the process diagram how it integrates. 
Regarding the SAP Fieldglass integration there is the possibility to replicate Workforce Persons from SAP Fieldglass via SAP MDI to SAP Project and Resource Management which can be enriched and being considered for staffing.

Best regards, Miguel

entry.title: Re: System temporarily unavailable error - C_THR81_2512 launch - Support closing tickets without res
entry.content: hi @Alexander_Rogalsky61 any news? trying to get my certification and 5th time meeting the same error. The support team answered its maitenence work due to some errors in new version. Firstly it was supposed to be available on june 17th, then last week, but not working for me toady neither. Please share if you get any update

entry.title: Security Administrator Exam
entry.content:
Planning on taking the SAP C_SEC certification? Here are a few tips that may help with your preparation:

  • Pay close attention to SAP naming conventions. Many exam questions rely on the exact terminology used by SAP, so understanding names, roles, objects, and concepts is essential.

  • Read each question carefully—more than once if needed. A single word can change the meaning of the question and lead to a different answer.

  • Think outside the box. Some questions are scenario-based and require you to apply your knowledge rather than simply recall facts.

  • Review the Live Sessions, Learning Journeys, and training materials thoroughly. They cover the key topics and concepts you'll need for the exam, so don't skip them.

Good luck with your preparation and the exam!

#saphana#fiori

entry.title: Re: Monitor Your Number Range Objects with SAP EarlyWatch Alert
entry.content:
Hello Experts,

Over the past two weeks, I’ve received a message in my Early Watch Alert stating that the number range for spool jobs is critically low. Our number range SPO_NUM is set from 100 to 99,000 and is configured to rolling.

Two days ago, the number 99,000 was reached, and the spool jobs started over at 100. Except for a few spool jobs that are not supposed to be deleted, we have enough space in the number range (standard deletion after 8 days).


The warning is missing again in the current Early Watch Alert. Am I correct in thinking that I can ignore this warning because of the number range is set to rolling?

Best regards,

Herbert

entry.title: Re: Error "No authorization to start the UI for transacti...
entry.content: Thanks @DipenShah. I would like to clarify that these are CBC configuration points, not Apps. So we cannot find them in IAM Fiori App. They are listed in "IMG Activity TRAN" associated to specific catalogs and as we explained first, they are not providing access or they are only associated to country-specific catalogs not active in our system. Do you know where can we find them? Thanks.

entry.title: Define a cost element category for account 5401001005 (controlling area PVM)
entry.content:
We are posting the Manua JV via F-02 t code and  getting error message : 

Define a cost element category for account 5401001005 (controlling area PVM)

Message No. KI280

Diagnosis

Account 5401001005 is a profit and loss account. You assigned it to an object in cost accounting (for example, a cost center, order, project, or profitability segment).

In this case, a cost element category must exist for account 5401001005 in the determined controlling area PVM.
See transaction FS00, "Control Data" area.

 

Procedure

Define a cost element category for account 5401001005 and post again or delete the auxiliary account assignment to objects in cost accounting.

With profitability segment assignment, the error message is only displayed when account-based profitability analysis is activated. Here it may be a good idea to suppress the error message and ignore the account assignment during the posting. This is particularly the case when the posting is not only relevant for the result from the usual busin

entry.title: Re: Configure Single-Sign-On for SAP Ariba with SAP Cloud Identity Services
entry.content:
@Cyril-Blandin   Thanks for the clear instructions. I do have a question though.

We are running a suite-integrated environment (SLP, Sourcing, CM, Procurement (parent and child)). When I check the systems and provisioning page in SAP4ME I only see two systems that are available for provisioning. Both of them are entitled SAP Ariba Supplier Management. Should we not also see the SAP Ariba Procurement systems here?

entry.title: Re: S4HC | Batch Check Printing
entry.content:
Hello,

Thank you very much for your question. 

Can you please check the following document?

Configuring the Output of Outgoing Checks

Kind regards,

Henrike

entry.title: Re: Decimal of USD in SAP public cloud
entry.content:
Dear @Moon3,

For open Purchase Order migration, the same principle applies. In Public Cloud the PO per-unit price is restricted to 2 decimal places so 0.025 USD / 1 PC cannot be migrated or displayed directly as a 3-decimal unit price in the standard PO field. The recommended approach is to convert the price to a larger price unit like above. This keeps the same commercial price mathematically and avoids the 2-decimal limitation

If the customer requires the standard PO to show exactly 0.025 USD / 1 PC this is not possible in standard Public Cloud. In that case the standard limitation needs to be accepted or the customer can submit an improvement request via SAP Customer Influence

More details below:

SAP KBA 2963059 - How to Submit Feature Requests for SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition

Best Regards,

Dawid

entry.title: Re: Session management and custom Field mappings in CDC
entry.content:
Hi @Ritumbhara 

Great questions! Thanks for sending these over. Let me address each one separately.

1. If CDC leads the session, does a CDC timeout also log the user out of Commerce?

Yes — but it's passive, not instant.

When you configure Session Lead = CDC/Gigya in Backoffice, here's what actually happens step by step:

  1. When the CDC session times out, the Gigya JS SDK automatically deletes the GLT cookie (gltoken_{apiKey}) from the browser.
  2. On the next request the user makes to Commerce/Hybris, a filter called GigyaLoginFilter runs and checks for that cookie.
  3. If the cookie is missing → it redirects to /logout on the Commerce/Hybris side, which invalidates the Commerce/Hybris session.

So both systems end up logged out — but the Commerce/Hybris logout is triggered on the next request, not the moment CDC times out. If the browser is idle and no request is made, the Commerce/Hybris server-side session will linger until either:

  • The user makes a new request (filter kicks in), or
  • Commerce/Hybris's own HTTP session timeout fires independently

Quick summary: CDC timeout → Commerce/Hybris logout, but not in real-time — it's detected passively via a missing cookie on the next request.

entry.title: Re: Periodenselektion nicht mehr möglich
entry.content:
Hallo,

ich habe mir den Bericht gerade in einem klassischen R/3 System angesehen - auch dirt sind Periode und Geschäftsjahr nicht in der Selektion enthalten.

Ich habe mir die Berichtsdefinition des zugehörigen Berichts 6L04-001 (Bibliothek 6O1) in der Transaktion GRR3 angesehen: Perioden und  Geschäftsjahr scheinen zwar grundsätzlich vorhanden zu sein, sind aber nicht als Variable aktiv (siehe Meüpfad Bearbeiten -> Allg. Selektionen).

Sie könnten den Bericht in der Transaktion GRR2 kopieren, Geschäftsjahr und Periode als Variable akivieren und mit entsprechenden Merkmalen belegen. Dann sollte die Auswahl von Perioden/dem Geschäftsjahr möglich sein.

Hoffe, meine Antwort hilft weiter. Mit freundlichen Grüßen.

entry.title: Re: June Developer Challenge - CAP Knowledge - Summary
entry.content:
It was a great learning.

Thanks @qmacro for organizing questions in such a way that we are able to explore the cap documentation a bit deeper than usual😀.

entry.title: Re: SAP S/4HANA – SAP Note 1489799 for BAPI_PR_CREATE (Su...
entry.content: Thank you for your clarification. We are using SAP S/4HANA, and we understand that the correction is already included. However, we still receive message ME154 when creating a subcontracting purchase requisition without a BOM. Could you please advise whether this behavior is standard in S/4HANA, or if there is another SAP Note or configuration that should be checked? Thank you in advance.

entry.title: Re: How the Advanced Success Plan for SAP CX Operationalizes Hyper-Personalization at Scale
entry.content: This is such an important topic for customers right now, AI will not help scale unless the interdependent layers of data, decisioning, and delivery are fixed first. This is where a continuous engagement like the Success Plan can really make a difference.

entry.title: Re: SAP S/4HANA – SAP Note 1489799 for BAPI_PR_CREATE (Su...
entry.content:
This is an old ECC note, obsolete since release 6, Ehp4.

Before raising a case for support, check the following KBA for guidance:

  • 2226222 - Error ME154 or no components included in a subcontract order using BAPI

entry.title: Re: Session management and custom Field mappings in CDC
entry.content:
Regarding the second question: How does Commerce know the CDC session has expired?

Short answer: It doesn't get notified — it detects it indirectly through the browser cookie.

There is no server-to-server push notification from CDC to Commerce/Hybris. The mechanism is purely cookie-based:

  • CDC's Web SDK deletes the GLT cookie when the session expires
  • GigyaLoginFilter running in Commerce/Hybris checks on every request: "Is the GLT cookie still there?"
  • If the cookie is gone → logout is triggered

That's the entire detection logic — just a null cookie check. 

This is a known trade-off of cookie-based session sync. It works well for normal browser usage but has a gap for idle sessions or headless/API clients that don't send cookies.

entry.title: Re: Alert Management Email Notification Not Received
entry.content:
First of all ... version of SAPB1?? 

To be able to guide you, the first thing we need is that information.

entry.title: Dunning letter generated without Customer line item
entry.content: Dunning letter generated without Customer line item

Wow!
Thank you @qmacro  for organizing this challenge. It was different from the past challenges we had. Capire is indeed maintained very beautifully. I sure had some difficulties finding answers for few questions, but I got to spend more time with Capire. Glad to participate and hope to be part of more. 😉

entry.title: Re: SAP Business One 10.0 - B1LOGGING Database RAM Usage
entry.content: In SAPB1 version 10.x In a system with microservices, some functions can be switched to manual instead of automatic if you don't use them. For example, the Integration Service.

entry.title: Re: Unable to Capture PO Number from Success Message in T...
entry.content: Good Day Chris

entry.title: Re: Recording one characteristic at multiple fixed measur...
entry.content: Hello Memem, Unfortunately, I don't think this solution would be sufficient for our scenario. Suppose I have a delivery consisting of 3 pieces of material. In that case, it won't be possible to collect 5 samples for the Diameter characteristic, even if I assign a sampling procedure requiring 5 samples to that MIC. SAP will only allow me to inspect the maximum number of available pieces, so only 3 result fields will be available instead of 5. Do you know if there is any way to use Inspection Points for only a single MIC? I tried using Free Inspection Points in the inspection plan header, but unfortunately this forces me to change the sampling procedure for all MICs in the inspection plan, not just the Diameter characteristic. I'm wondering if there is any other standard way to handle this requirement.

entry.title: SAP RDP: ES Plastic Tax for non-EU imports
entry.content:
Dear community,

I would like some clarification on the extract of the report documentation from SAP Responsible Design and Production below. My understanding is that goods receipts from suppliers outside of EU are not relevant for the Spanish Plastic Tax (only for inventory balancing). In the report documentation seems to be a mistake saying that the supplier of the non-EU import should be from a EU member state (see red text below). Could you please confirm how the Spanish Plastic Tax considers imports from non-EU countries?

Import Non-EU (IMPORT)

This logistic process is used with the logistic processes GR_ST_IMP and GR_PO_IMP to display the reporting data in the Analyze Reporting Data app and for the packaging inventory balance.

No

Yes

  • The Posting Date Date must be within the validity period of the report configuration and reporting period.

  • The Plant Country ISO Code must be Spain.

  • For logistic process GR_ST_IMP, the Issuing or Receiving Plant Country ISO Code must be an European Union member state other than Spain.

  • For logistic process GR_PO_IMP, the Supplier Country must be a European Union member state, the Supplier Country ISO Code must not be Spain, and supplier is not liable for plastic tax.

    • You can check the existing goods with IncoTerms and whether the supplier is liable for plastic tax in the Configuration Data app.

  • The QuantityInBaseUnit attribute is maintained as described in Material Documents.

Best regards,

Jessy

entry.title: Re: Session management and custom Field mappings in CDC
entry.content:
Hi @Ritumbhara 

About the 3600s configuration in the Spartacus CDC library setup, this hard-coded value will be passed to CDC from the JS storefront application. 

So, your understanding is correct — it's the session duration in seconds.

This value is the sessionExpiration config for CDC, either passed to the CDC showScreenSet() call when rendering a login screen or passed to the CDC accounts.login() API for headless login, and it controls how long the CDC/Gigya session token stays valid after login.

When a user logs in (either through a CDC Screen Set or a direct CDC API call), this value is passed to the CDC Web SDK to set the session lifetime. So with 3600s, the user's CDC session will expire after 1 hour of inactivity or elapsed time, depending on the session type.

Is it related to session management? — Yes, directly. This is the value that feeds into the session lifecycle we discussed:

  • It sets the CDC session duration
  • Once it expires, the GLT cookie gets cleared
  • Which then triggers the Commerce/Hybris logout on the next request (as explained in the previous question)

You can configure this per site in your Spartacus CDC config, and it falls back to 3600s (1 hour) as the default if not explicitly set.

entry.title: Re: Choosing the Right Component When Opening a Support Case for SAP GTS Edition for Hana
entry.content:
Hi Alex,

Thanks so much the comment and feedback. 

The community tag SAP Global Trade Services remains for both the GTS and GTS edition for Hana  products.

But choosing the right component in sap for me, will help ensure that the most relevant knowledge sources (KBAs, notes etc) are displayed to the user before opening a case, and may even avoid the case being opened in the first place. 

FYI Also there's a list of where to find information on GTS (eg. UA, live sessions, where to submit enhancement requests etc)  outlined in KBA 2447201 - Where to find GTS Documentation.
Hope that helps. 

Thanks 

Ann Marie

entry.title: Add auxiliary packaging material to an existing Handling unit in the Warehouse via API
entry.content:
Hi,
We would like to add auxiliary packaging material to an existing WM Handling unit. 
I found in the constraints that we can't create an HU with Auxiliary packaging material, but it doesn't explicit we can't add them. 
Handling Unit | SAP Help Portal 

In parallel I also created a customer influence ticket for it: 
https://influence.sap.com/sap/ino/#/idea/377179

Kr Jeroen

entry.title: Re: I need guidance on handling audit requirements for test management in SAP Cloud ALM.
entry.content: Hi, please explain your requirement in more detail. Regards, Walburga

entry.title: Re: Setup a pricing configuration for aggregated usage ch...
entry.content:
Hi, 
the subscription id is not a standard field for pricing and is therefore not available in the pricing context. See the list of standard fields here. Also, usage record item fields are only available for individually rated usage records, not for aggregated ones. 

Basically there are two options (see Customized Pricing ) 

  1. customize the price of the subscription using an overridable field
  2. make use of a custom reference to influence pricing 

The second option is better suited if you have an attribute which influences the calculated price for multiple subscriptions in the same way. The first option is probably the one you are looking for. 
The automatic pricing check for rate plans in the product app shows the information in case additional attributes are used to calculate a price, because typically the actual subscription data is not available then. 

Please check out also these blogs:

  • Pricing based on custom attributes with SAP Subscription Billing
  • Customizing the Pricing of a Subscription

entry.title: Creating an SAP SD Billing Document Using a BAPI in SAP Build and Accessing It Through 'Joule Work'
entry.content:
Hi Everyone!

Recently, I was working on a requirement to create an SD billing document in SAP S/4HANA using SAP Build Process Automation and make it accessible through the Joule Work mobile app.

Due to some internal requirements & technical restrictions, the only option available to me was to use the BAPI_BILLINGDOC_CREATEMULTIPLE BAPI to create the billing document.

I am sharing the following demo which I created in the test environment. To use this BAPI in SAP Build, there are two main configurations required:

1. Map the virtual host to the internal host in SAP Cloud Connector using the RFC protocol, and expose the required BAPI or RFC function module.

2. Create a destination of type RFC in the SAP BTP Cockpit.

Let's first see how Cloud to On-Premises mapping in Cloud Connector looks like:

We also need to enable the required resources, such as the BAPIs and function modules that we want to consume in SAP Build. The RFC_FUNCTION_SEARCH function module enables the search for function modules from SAP Build.



And this is how the destination in BTP looks:



The virtual message server is configured in the Cloud Connector and mapped to the internal SAP S/4HANA host, and logon group information is available in SMLG.



Logon group can be found here :



Now that the Cloud Connector and BTP destination configurations are complete, let’s log in to SAP Build and see how we can create an action using BAPI_BILLINGDOC_CREATEMULTIPLE.

Select RFC's, BAPI's:



All the BAPIs & RFCs from backend will be available to use. We will go ahead and look for BAPI/RFC we'd like to use, and create a new project.



The action is now available in Connectors -> Actions, and can be used in our build projects.



Using this action, I created a custom process that can be triggered to generate a billing document by entering the reference sales document number.



Let's see it in action;
Assume we have below sales order, for which we'd like to generate a billing document.



Let’s now access the custom process we created. I have added the form URL to SAP Build Work Zone for easy access.





Since I added an email notification step to the process, we receive an email once the billing document is generated successfully.



Let's validated the generated billing document number in the system.



To access SAP Build Work Zone applications and tiles in the Joule mobile app, open Settings, scan the QR code, and complete the authentication process.





As we can see below, all our SAP Build Work Zone applications are now accessible through the Joule Work mobile app as well.





Thanks for reading!

Stay tuned for more demos on AI agents using Joule Studio, n8n, and automation in SAP Build.

entry.title: PO Print Output – Chinese Material Description Showing as ###
entry.content: When printing a Purchase Order, the material description maintained in Chinese language is not displayed correctly. Instead of the Chinese text, the output shows “###”

entry.title: Re: June Developer Challenge - CAP Knowledge!
entry.content:
Hey Hi, 

sorry didn't check out this month's challenge, surely will go through and try to answer all.

entry.title: Re: Blogpost Approval Timelines
entry.content:
Hello,

We've been experiencing so unexpected delays related to blog posts moderation. The post was now approved.

Thank you for posting on the community and for your patience :).

Regards,

Moshe

entry.title: Joule Work mobile app Version 3.1 is out
entry.content:
With the latest release of Joule Work mobile app Version 3.1, the app provides exciting features for both, the New Experience (Engagement Layer), as well as the SAP Build Work Zone Experience – formally called SAP Mobile Start.

Let me start with the features that are valid for both experiences.
Based on customer feedback, the app allows you now to customise the Support Section on the Settings screen. This allows you to add additional information for your users, in case they have any support needs. Administrators can easily add information, such as phone numbers, email addresses, and websites for further end user support. Once configured, the info is displayed in the Profile and Settings screen as well as during the onboarding process. The feature can be added and configured via SAP Mobile Services or via Mobile Device Management (MDM). Please also check our SAP Help page for details.

We also have updated the supported languages for SAP Joule Work and added: Greek, Croatian, Indonesian, Romanian, Ukrainian, and Vietnamese.

Now, let us have a look at the features that are relevant for the SAP Build Work Zone Experience.
First and most important, you can now directly access workspaces from SAP Build Work Zone, advanced edition - via a dedicated screen that is available in the navigation bar. This ensures that you get direct access to your most needed content from anywhere in the app. In case you work with many workspaces, you can easily filter them, for instance if you only want to see your favourites – or search for them. As soon as this feature got enabled by the admin,  the SAP Build Work Zone Workspaces screen will be visible.  

Screenshot: SAP Build Work Zone, advanced edition – Workspaces

Second, users can now change the language of external SAP Build Work Zone tiles, like news and to-dos directly inside the app, from the settings screen.

Now, let us have a look at the new features for our Joule Work New Experience.
As introduced in my last blog “Joule Work, mobile app is here”, Spaces are one of the key elements of the New Experience. Lets recap: Spaces are dynamic, intent-driven workspaces for more complex business problems. They are composed in real time to bring the right data from different systems, tools, and context together for a specific goal, freeing people from working in multiple applications and passive UIs. With the new release, you can now pin your favourite spaces to the top of the spaces list, what will make it much easier for you to access your favourite content. To do so, just do a long tap on the space that you want to pin and the menu appears. Here, you can easily rename your space, delete it or pin/ unpin it. Joule Work New Experience is currently available within an Early Adapter Care program.

 Screenshot: Pin favourite Spaces to the top of the list

I hope you will enjoy these features in the Joule Work mobile app.

Visit SAP Mobile Experience Community Page and click “follow” to get the latest development and innovation of our solutions. We look forward to hearing about your experience with setting up the solution in your landscape; please do share your thoughts and comments below. Enter here for additional questions regarding SAP Mobile Experience Applications.

Want to be notified? Check your profile settings to ensure you have your settings activated.

entry.title: Standard API required for Manage Workforce App
entry.content:
We need a standard API to fetch the work agreement details ( Work agreement ID, Start Date and End Date ) and Worker Types details(Worker type, start date and End Date ) from the standard app "MANAGE WORKFORCE" .


--- Support Assistant ---
Q: Please categorize your issue by choosing the option below that best fits your scenario.
A: Edit worker data
Q: Please categorize your issue by choosing the option below that best fits your scenario.
A: Manage Workforce


------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------
Learn more about the SAP Support user and program here.

entry.title: Re: Standard API required for Manage Workforce App
entry.content:
Dear Customer,

 

You can use the API "Replicate Basic Master Data for Workforce from External HR Systems" for fetching the data for the Worker maintained in the application Manage Workforce.

The below nodes contains the required data for the respective purpose:

WorkAgreementInformation | SAP Help Portal -> It stores the data for the Worker Type in the field PersonWorkAgreementType and the Work agreement Ids in the field (PersonWorkAgreementUUID)

ValidityPeriod | SAP Help Portal -> It stores the start date and the end date for the Work agreements which maps to the start and the end date of the Worker Type as well.

 

Thanks and Regards,
Moumita.

entry.title: AMT_BASE is NOT directly mapped to VBSEGS-FWBAS in BAPI bapi_acc_document_post
entry.content:
I am uploading G/L account lines as an accounting document using BAPI_ACC_DOCUMENT_POST.
I tried to pass AMT_BASE to BAPI_ACC_DOCUMENT_POST, but no value is being updated in the FWBAS field of VBSEGS.
I also tried filling other fields such as AMT_BASE_LONG and TAX_AMT, but it still does not work.
Is there anything I might be missing?

entry.title: Re: UI5 Plugins for Coding Agents: Now Supporting GitHub Copilot
entry.content: @shravan_pishike Looks like your marketplace is outdated. Checkout https://github.com/UI5/plugins-coding-agents/issues/11#issuecomment-4175299259 to update the marketplace.

entry.title: Re: On‑the‑Fly Excel Download in a RAP Fiori App — No Database Table, No Persistence, Pure CDS + ABA
entry.content: Hi @Reserve - Short answer is 'NO'. RAP action is not primarily meant to enable the file download and the action result set is not respected by the Fiori framework to download a file. Work around: You can use this Custom Entity in the service definition and with the help of a UI breakout, you can call this custom entity to download the attachment.

entry.title: Re: Extra objects being populated in the Calibration PM O...
entry.content: Hello. Apologies. I checked again and could see that the maintenance plan item has these extra equipments. However there are old PM orders which were created from same plan and they don’t have those extra equipments in them. I don’t see any change documents for the maintenance plan items also.

entry.title: Re: June Developer Challenge - CAP Knowledge - Summary
entry.content: I can only second @Joery's comment. Being mostly an ABAP developer myself, it was a nice but beginner friendly challenge to dig through the CAP documentation. 😊 Thanks @qmacro!

entry.title: Re: Attachments in Quality Notification emails are not in...
entry.content:
Dear Aristotle Santos,

thank you for the details provided. It worth to open a case to SAP Support related to this system response.

I wish you a nice day,
​Albert
​***
​Read and follow the 'SAP Community Rules of Engagement' at
​https://www.sap.com/community/about/rules-of-engagement.html

entry.title: Re: Joule A2A: Propagate the User Identity from Joule into S/4HANA
entry.content: Hi @M_M_J, you are on a good track here. As the title suggests this is mainly for Public Cloud. The pattern for PCE and OnPrem are similar in a sense that a) you need to activate the ODATA Services first https://me.sap.com/notes/0002595454 here you just use the classical "/IWFND/MAINT_SERVICE" instead of the Fiori Apps b) ensure connectivity via the Cloud Connector https://help.sap.com/docs/connectivity/sap-btp-connectivity-cf/scenario-cloud-to-on-premise and c) configure the token flow from Joule to the PCE system https://help.sap.com/docs/connectivity/sap-btp-connectivity-cf/configure-principal-propagation-via-ias-token There are some slight differences - while S4 Public is using Oauth2SAMLBearer - we use the PrincipalPropagation Destination Type here. In deed I am looking to create something like this in another blog soon, but currently I am still awaiting getting my hands on a system 🙂

entry.title: Re: Recording one characteristic at multiple fixed measur...
entry.content:
Greetings,

well in this case you can use 100% inspection or try to define sampling plan but these depend on different delivery quantity. In case your delivery quantity is 1000 PC you cannot expect users to input 1000 results but if you know that deliveries are alays in 1, 3 , 7, 13 PC (finite combinations) you can prepare a sampling plan based on the quantity.

Best regards, M.

entry.title: Re: June Developer Challenge - CAP Knowledge - Summary
entry.content:
It was a fantastic challenge.
The questions are didactically brilliant and perfect tailored to the also brilliant Capire-Docu.
I have learned a lot.
